title: Melinia micrantha
intro: Melinia micrantha, commonly known as Pilocarpus, is a small, shrub-like plant belonging to the Rutaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Melinia micrantha include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Melinia micrantha is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including hypertension and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Melinia micrantha is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Melinia, which is a group of plants endemic to tropical regions. Within the Rubiaceae family, Melinia micrantha is part of the subfamily Rubioideae. The subfamily Rubioideae is further divided into several tribes, with Melinia micrantha likely belonging to the tribe Spermaeae. The precise classification of Melinia micrantha may be subject to ongoing research and updates in botanical taxonomy.
